latest.title: Further CEO Share Purchase
latest.content:
13 Feb 2026 14:30
RNS Number : 0039T
Renalytix PLC
13 February 2026
Renalytix plc
("Renalytix" or the "Company")
Further CEO Share Purchase
LONDON and NEW YORK, 13 February 2026
- Renalytix plc (LSE: RENX) (OTCQB: RNLXY), a precision medicine diagnostics company, whose product
kidneyintelX.dkd
, is the only FDA-approved and Medicare-reimbursed prognostic test to support early-stage risk assessment in chronic diabetic kidney disease,
announces
that on 12 February 2026 James McCullough
, Chief Executive Officer, bought a further 750 American Depositary Shares (ADS) at an average price of US$3.15 per ADS.
ADS consist of 50 Ordinary Shares of £0.0025 each.
Following these purchases,
James McCullough
is interested in 3,430,096 Ordinary Shares1 representing 0.78% of the issued share capital of the Company.
1
James McCullough shareholding includes ordinary shares held through his family trust, The McCullough 2020 Irrevocable Trust.
For further information, please contact:
Renalytix plc
www.renalytix.com
James McCullough, CEO
Via Walbrook PR
SP Angel Corporate Finance LLP (Nominated Adviser and Joint Broker)
Tel:
+44 (0)20 3470 0470
Jeff Keating / David Hignell
(Corporate Finance)
Vadim Alexandre (Corporate Broking)
Oberon Capital (Joint Broker)
Tel: +44 (0)20 3179 5300
Mike Seabrook / Nick Lovering
Walbrook PR Limited
Tel: +44 (0)20 7933 8780 or
renalytix@walbrookpr.com
Paul McManus / Alice Woodings
Mob: +44 (0)7980 541 893 / +44 (0)7407 804 654
About Renalytix
(
www.renalytix.com
)
Renalytix (LSE: RENX) (OTCQB: RNLXY) is an artificial intelligence-enabled
in vitro
diagnostics company, focused on optimizing clinical management of kidney disease to drive improved patient outcomes. Renalytix has received FDA approval and Medicare reimbursement for
kidneyintelX.dkd
which is now offered commercially in the United States.
Unrecognized and uncontrolled diabetic kidney disease remains one of the largest barriers to controlling cost and suffering in the United States and the United Kingdom's medical system, affecting approximately 15 million and 8 million people, respectively. After five years of development and clinical validation,
kidneyintelX.dkd
is the only FDA-approved and Medicare-reimbursed prognostic tool capable of understanding a patient's risk with diabetic kidney disease early where treatment has maximal effect.
kidneyintelX.dkd
is now being deployed across large physician group practices and health systems in select regions of the United States.
The over 15,000 patients that have been tested by
kidneyintelX.dkd
have produced a substantial body of real-world performance data. In patient populations where
kidneyintelX.dkd
has been deployed, a demonstrated and significant increase in diagnosis, prognosis, and treatment rates have been recorded.
kidneyintelX.dkd
now has full reimbursement established by Medicare, the largest insurance payer in the United States, at $950 per reportable result.
kidneyintelX.dkd
is also recommended for use in the international chronic kidney disease clinical guidelines (KDIGO).
KidneyIntelX
is based on technology developed by Mount Sinai faculty and licensed to Renalytix AI, Inc. Mount Sinai faculty members are co-founders and equity owners in the Company. In addition, the Icahn School of Medicine at Mount Sinai has equity ownership in Renalytix.
For information about the kidneyintelX,dkd test, visit
kidneyintelx.com
.

company_30s.what_they_do: Renalytix plc develops and sells diagnostic tests and digital decision‑support tools that help doctors spot and manage kidney disease earlier than standard practice.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Kidney disease is a growing global health problem that costs billions in treatment and lost productivity. Renalytix’s tests give clinicians a cheaper, faster way to identify high‑risk patients and tailor care, giving the company a potentially large addressable market and a clear competitive edge over generic lab tests.
company_30s.current_state: The firm is still in a high‑burn phase, relying on a modest cash runway that extends only to the middle of the year. Share price has collapsed sharply, but the company has recently secured a controlling stake from a private equity investor and is reporting incremental clinical integrations that may begin generating revenue.

rns_narrative: Renalytix has been busy managing its capital base – a private‑equity stake was taken, shares were bought by insiders, and a director’s share purchase was announced.  These moves suggest the firm is trying to shore up finances and keep the business alive.
The company’s trading update confirms that cash reserves will last until roughly June, giving it a short‑term runway but also highlighting the urgency of new funding or revenue streams.

A series of clinical integration announcements – three new kidney‑intelligent platforms now being deployed in hospitals – signals that the product is gaining traction, which could be a catalyst for a price bounce once the market recognises the value.

crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A few voices highlight the new equity stake and the hospital integrations, suggesting the company could finally break out of its slump.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Others point to the steep cash burn, the share price collapse and the fact that the firm has yet to generate sustainable revenue, warning that the downturn could continue.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The mood has shifted from outright pessimism to a cautious optimism after the private‑equity deal, but overall engagement remains low.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for the ticker is flat – no public search activity, which means a news event could generate a surprise move.

near_term_catalysts: A formal partnership announcement with a major hospital system that would lock in a sizeable volume of diagnostic orders.
Any new funding round that extends the cash runway beyond the current June horizon.
Regulatory clearance for a new product line that can be marketed in the next quarter.
A volume spike on the stock – a sudden influx of shares traded that would break the current low‑volume plateau.
expect_next.watch_for: Key signals to monitor over the next 2–3 months are a sustained increase in daily volume, the first positive RNS about a new partnership or funding, and a price move that touches the 3p support level with a breakout above it.
expect_next.timing_regime: The share has already fallen well beyond 70% of its peak, indicating capitulation is likely complete. The APEX compression score suggests a tight spring that could snap back once a catalyst arrives. The window for mean reversion is therefore in the next 60–90 days if a catalyst materialises.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The stock is deep‑crashed – a drop of 70–95% puts it in the highest‑risk, highest‑reward zone of our system. If the new hospital integrations or a funding announcement materialises, the price could recover to roughly half of its previous peak, delivering a 200–300% upside. The asymmetry is strong: a modest downside risk versus a large upside potential.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: If the cash burn continues unchecked and no new funding or revenue streams materialise, the company could run out of money and fail to deliver on its product roadmap, driving the share price back to zero. The worst‑case loss is the total capital invested.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss is 100% of the position if the company collapses. Potential gain could be 3–4 times that amount if the price rebounds to mid‑levels. The risk‑reward ratio is therefore roughly 1:3 to 1:4.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a dip near the 3p support level, sizing the position to 10% of your total portfolio. Place a stop just below the low of the support zone to limit downside. Target a 2–3× return if the price recovers to mid‑levels.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for a volume spike that exceeds the 10‑day average by at least 2×, or for a clear RNS announcing a new partnership or funding. These events are the most likely to trigger the bounce.
playbook.if_passing: If the company files for bankruptcy, is delisted, or the share price falls to zero, cut losses immediately and reallocate capital elsewhere.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Renalytix’s deep crash, coupled with a potential catalyst from new clinical integrations or private‑equity funding, creates a high‑asymmetry contrarian opportunity – a steep upside if the price rebounds, but a complete loss if the company fails to secure its runway.
